vuln_q_0031_0000.title: Information exposure through an exception
vuln_q_0031_0000.description: [Stack trace information](1) flows to this location and may be exposed to an external user.

vuln_q_0031_0000.title: Flask app is run in debug mode
vuln_q_0031_0000.description: A Flask app appears to be run in debug mode. This may allow an attacker to run arbitrary code through the debugger.

vuln_q_0017_0001.title: Reflected server-side cross-site scripting
vuln_q_0017_0001.description: Cross-site scripting vulnerability due to a [user-provided value](1).

vuln_q_0017_0001.title: Construction of a cookie using user-supplied input
vuln_q_0017_0001.description: Cookie is constructed from a [user-supplied input](1).

vuln_q_0069_0000.title: Use of a broken or weak cryptographic algorithm
vuln_q_0069_0000.description:
[The block mode ECB](1) is broken or weak, and should not be used.
[The cryptographic algorithm DES](2) is broken or weak, and should not be used.

vuln_q_0096_0001.title: Use of a broken or weak cryptographic hashing algorithm on sensitive data
vuln_q_0096_0001.description:
[Sensitive data (password)](1) is used in a hashing algorithm (SHA256) that is insecure for password hashing, since it is not a computationally expensive hash function.
[Sensitive data (password)](2) is used in a hashing algorithm (SHA256) that is insecure for password hashing, since it is not a computationally expensive hash function.

vuln_q_0025_0000.title: Use of insecure SSL/TLS version
vuln_q_0025_0000.description:
Insecure SSL/TLS protocol version TLSv1 allowed by [call to ssl.create_default_context](1).
Insecure SSL/TLS protocol version TLSv1_1 allowed by [call to ssl.create_default_context](1).
